title: Thelypteris limbata
intro: Thelypteris limbata, also known as the Mexican shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris limbata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Thelypteris limbata is commonly used to make herbal teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as poultices and salves, to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Thelypteris limbata is a species of fern within the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which includes about 150 species of terrestrial ferns. The Thelypteridaceae family consists of about 1,000 species of ferns found primarily in tropical and subtropical regions. These ferns are typically characterized by their pinnately compound leaves and are often found in shaded, humid environments. Thelypteris limbata's classification reflects its unique characteristics and its place within the larger group of terrestrial ferns.
